meta.title: BrowsePad – TV-First Web Browser for Android TV
meta.description: BrowsePad is a TV-first web browser for Android TV with tabs, bookmarks, history, desktop view, and phone-based remote control through BrowsePad Connect.
meta.keywords: BrowsePad, Android TV browser, TV web browser, browser for Android TV, Google TV browser, Fire TV browser, TV remote browser, Android TV tabs, BrowsePad Connect, phone remote browser, Android TV bookmarks, Android TV history, desktop mode browser TV, Android TV downloads, Leanback browser
meta.author: Deepak PK

og.title: BrowsePad for Android TV
og.description: Browse the web on Android TV with tabs, an app-wide pointer, and phone-based remote control through BrowsePad Connect.

twitter.title: BrowsePad for Android TV
twitter.description: A TV-first Android TV browser with tabs, bookmarks, history, desktop view, and a built-in mobile remote experience.

product.name: BrowsePad
product.heroTitle: BrowsePad – A TV-First Browser for Android TV
product.heroDescription: Browse the web on Android TV, Google TV, and Fire TV with a desktop-style interface, multi-tab support, an app-wide pointer, and BrowsePad Connect for phone-based trackpad control.

cards.title: Made for Android TV
bullets: Desktop-style browser shell optimized for TV screens
Works with Android TV, Google TV, and Fire TV style remote navigation
Smooth handoff between toolbar focus mode and pointer mode
Browser chrome can auto-hide while browsing and return when needed

cards.title: Tabs, Bookmarks, and History
bullets: Create, switch, close, and close all tabs from a TV-friendly tab strip
Per-tab titles and favicons make open pages easy to scan
Save bookmarks from the toolbar and review them in a dedicated Bookmarks page
Automatic browsing history with clear-history confirmation and session restore

cards.title: Smart Address Bar and Search
bullets: Address bar accepts full URLs, bare domains, and search queries
Automatic search-vs-URL resolution keeps navigation simple on TV
Choose your default search engine from Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Brave Search, Perplexity, or Andi
Built-in `browsepad://connect` route opens the BrowsePad Connect experience instantly

cards.title: TV Pointer and Remote Controls
bullets: Visible app-wide pointer works across browser chrome, internal pages, and website content
Use the D-pad for precise pointer nudging or hold directions for continuous movement
Long-press OK triggers secondary or right-click behavior
Pointer edge scrolling helps reach content inside long web pages

cards.title: BrowsePad Connect
bullets: Built-in Connect page is available as a native TV tab
QR code pairing makes it fast to connect a phone
Controller URL is shown on the TV for manual pairing when needed
Connection status, disconnect summaries, and port fallback improve reliability

cards.title: Phone-Based Remote Input
bullets: Phone controller supports single-finger cursor movement and tap-to-click
Use two-finger gestures for right-click and scrolling, plus fling momentum
Pinch-to-zoom and phone-side pointer speed settings help with difficult sites
Remote text input works for the address bar, webpage fields, textareas, and content-editable areas

cards.title: View Modes and Settings
bullets: Switch each site between Adaptive and Desktop view modes
Set a default view mode for sites without a saved override
Settings include search engine choice, cache clear, and app version display
Support pages like Help, Privacy Policy, Terms, About, and feedback open inside the browser

cards.title: Downloads and Web Handling
bullets: Download interception asks for confirmation before starting a file download
Android DownloadManager integration provides start and completion feedback
Context menu actions include open in new tab, copy link, and download resource
Friendly internal error pages cover common failures like timeouts, SSL issues, and connection errors

cards.title: Store and Subscription Support
bullets: Separate builds support Google Play, Amazon Appstore, and Huawei AppGallery
Store-specific subscription flows are integrated for each distribution channel
Subscription management entry points are available from the app menu
Recent subscription verification is cached with an offline grace period

items.title: Living Room Browsing
items.text: Use a TV as a real browser for news, video sites, web apps, and everyday browsing without struggling through phone-style controls.

items.title: Phone-as-Trackpad Setups
items.text: Pair a phone over your local network and control the TV browser with gestures, text input, scrolling, and pinch-to-zoom.

items.title: Search and Research on TV
items.text: Switch between search engines, open multiple tabs, save bookmarks, and revisit pages from history on a big screen.

items.title: Desktop-Style Site Access
items.text: Force desktop view for sites that work better with a traditional browser layout and keep per-site preferences where supported.

items.a: BrowsePad is a TV-first web browser for Android TV with tabs, bookmarks, history, desktop view support, and a built-in phone-based remote experience called BrowsePad Connect.

items.q: How do I control BrowsePad on a TV?
items.a: You can use a regular TV remote with the D-pad and OK button, or pair a phone through BrowsePad Connect for trackpad-style control, scrolling, zooming, and text input.
Yes. BrowsePad supports creating, switching, closing, and closing all tabs, with per-tab titles and favicons shown in the tab strip.

items.q: Can I use my phone as a remote?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad Connect serves a mobile controller from the TV app over your local network. The phone controller supports cursor movement, click, right-click, scrolling, pinch-to-zoom, and remote text entry.

items.q: What search engines can I choose from?
items.a: BrowsePad includes Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Brave Search, Perplexity, and Andi as built-in default search engine options.

items.q: Can websites open in desktop mode?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad supports per-site view mode switching between Adaptive and Desktop, and you can also choose a default view mode in settings.

items.q: Does BrowsePad save bookmarks and history?
items.a: Yes. You can save bookmarks from the toolbar, review them in a dedicated Bookmarks page, and revisit previously opened pages from History. Session restore also brings back previously open non-internal tabs after relaunch.

items.q: Can I download files from websites?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad intercepts downloads, asks for confirmation before starting them, and integrates with Android DownloadManager where supported.

items.q: What happens when a page fails to load?
items.a: BrowsePad shows a friendly internal error page and handles common failures such as host lookup problems, connection failures, timeouts, SSL failures, redirect loops, I/O issues, and unsupported schemes.

howItWorks.title: Start Browsing on TV in Minutes

steps.title: Open BrowsePad
steps.text: Launch BrowsePad on your Android TV, Google TV, or Fire TV device to start with a browser interface designed for large screens and remote input.

steps.title: Enter a URL or Search
steps.text: Use the address bar for direct URLs, bare domains, or search queries. BrowsePad automatically resolves whether you meant a website address or a search.

steps.title: Pair a Phone if You Want
steps.text: Open BrowsePad Connect to pair a phone with a QR code and use it as a trackpad remote with scrolling, zooming, and text input.

features.title: Why TV Users Will Like It

cards.title: TV-Friendly Tabs
bullets: Open, switch, and close tabs from a tab strip built for TV viewing
Favicons and titles keep multiple open pages easy to manage

cards.title: Pointer-Based Browsing
bullets: Visible pointer overlay works across both browser UI and webpage content
Remote and phone controls make hard-to-target sites easier to use

cards.title: BrowsePad Connect
bullets: Connect a phone quickly with QR pairing over your local network
Use gesture-based cursor control, scrolling, and remote text input

cards.title: Useful Browser Controls
bullets: Switch per-site view mode between Adaptive and Desktop
Save bookmarks, review history, and clear cached web data from settings

items.title: Living Room Browsing
items.text: Navigate websites comfortably on a TV with tabs, bookmarks, and a pointer-based interface.

items.title: Phone Remote Control
items.text: Pair a phone for trackpad gestures, zooming, scrolling, and text input when a regular remote is not enough.

items.title: Big-Screen Search
items.text: Search the web, compare pages across tabs, and revisit past pages with bookmarks and history.

items.title: Desktop-Site Access
items.text: Force desktop rendering for sites that behave better with a desktop-style browser user agent.

items.a: BrowsePad is a TV-first web browser for Android TV that combines tabs, pointer-based browsing, bookmarks, history, and phone-based remote control through BrowsePad Connect.

items.q: How do I browse if a normal TV remote feels limited?
items.a: BrowsePad includes both D-pad browsing and an app-wide pointer. You can also pair a phone over your local network and use BrowsePad Connect as a trackpad-style remote.
Yes. BrowsePad supports tab creation, tab switching, tab closing, and close-all-tabs support.

items.q: Can websites request a new window?
items.a: Yes. Sites that try to open a new browser window are handled by opening that content in a new tab.

items.q: Does BrowsePad support bookmarks and history?
items.a: Yes. Bookmarks and history each have dedicated internal pages, and BrowsePad also restores previously open non-internal tabs after relaunch.

items.q: Can I switch sites into desktop mode?
items.a: Yes. BrowsePad supports per-site switching between Adaptive and Desktop view modes, plus a default view mode setting.
Yes. BrowsePad confirms downloads before starting them and uses Android DownloadManager integration where supported.

items.q: What if a page fails or has SSL issues?
items.a: BrowsePad blocks insecure certificate flows and shows a friendly internal error page for common failures like timeouts, SSL problems, redirect loops, and host lookup errors.
BrowsePad Connect is built into the browser as an internal page, including a native TV Connect tab with QR pairing and controller status details.
